title: Public Resources Without Explicit Internet Ingress in Architecture Diagram
description: Architecture diagram contains resources with public IPs, public access policies, or internet-facing indicators, but lacks explicit Internet node or ingress connections. This hides the public attack surface from security reviewers.

implementation.description: Detect resources with public access indicators that aren't connected to Internet node in diagram
logic_steps: 1. Query resources with public IP addresses
3. Query load balancers with internal = false
4. Check if Internet node exists in diagram
5. Verify all public resources have Internet → Resource connections

remediation: Add Internet node to architecture diagram when public resources exist
Create explicit connections: Internet → PublicResource with red styling
Use InternetExposureDetector (Scripts/Generate/internet_exposure_detector.py) to identify public resources
Add security warning annotation: 'Public Attack Surface' on public resource edges
In generate_hierarchical_diagram.py render_connections(), ensure Internet → Public edges are always created
references: ArchitectureValidationAgent.md - Internet Ingress Detection (CRITICAL for Security)

examples.bad: diagram shows App Service, Function App, SQL Server but no Internet node; reviewers can't see public surface
examples.good: diagram shows: Internet → App Service → SQL; red edges indicate public access; clear attack surface

automation.validation_rule: Ensure has_internet = True when exposed_resources is non-empty
